{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 114 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x 11.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 114 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x 11.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tiers

The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 114 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x 11.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 114 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x 11.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|View from the west 750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Kurohimeyama Tumulus001.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 114 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x 11.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tiers

The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In aqqition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In aqqition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In aqqition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In aqqition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Tumulus historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the burial mound have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In aqqition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In aqqition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 114 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x 11.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

In aqqition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In aqqition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In aqqition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 114 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x 11.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In aqqition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In aqqition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 114 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x 11.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|View from the west 750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Kurohimeyama Kofun001.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In a11ition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In a11ition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In a11ition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition[[Category:addition]], 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition[[Category:addition]], there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition[[Category:addition]], 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition[[Category:addition]], there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|View from the west 750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Kurohimeyama Kofun001.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition[[Category:addition]], 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition[[Category:addition]], there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition[[Category:addition]], 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition[[Category:addition]], there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
