The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition[[Category:addition]], 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition[[Category:addition]], there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition[[Category:addition]], 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition[[Category:addition]], there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition[[Category:addition]], 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition[[Category:addition]], there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara-ku]], [[Sakai, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|View from the west 750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Kurohimeyama Kofun001.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kurohimeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/kurohimeyama.html Sakai City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://osaka-info.jp/spot/kurohigeyama-tumulus/ Osaka Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[Sakai, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=黒姫山古墳 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[美原区]]黒山 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |築造年代=5世紀中頃 |規模=全長114[[メートル|m]]、高さ11m |被葬者=丹比氏 |史跡=[[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）国指定 |有形文化財=出土甲冑類（堺市指定文化財） |出土品=[[甲冑]]、[[鉄刀]]、鉄[[剣]]、鉄[[鏃]]ほか多数 |画像=[[画像:黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg|250px]]
後円部（東から） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=黒姫山古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=44.21|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=27.53|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}} }} [[画像:黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|thumb|270px|前方部（西から）]] '''黒姫山古墳'''（くろひめやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[美原区]]黒山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定され{{Cite web|和書|author=文化観光局文化部文化財課|url=http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/kurohimeyama.html|title=黒姫山古墳|work=堺市|date=2020-04-03|accessdate=2020-10-16}}、出土甲冑類は堺市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|270px|right|{{center|黒姫山古墳の空中写真（1975年）}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[百舌鳥古墳群]]と[[古市古墳群]]の中間に位置する前方後円墳で、前方部は西側を向いている。大きさは、全長114[[メートル]]、後円部は直径64メートル・高さ11メートル、前方部は幅65メートル、高さ11.5メートルである。北側くびれ部に[[造り出し]]があり、墳丘の周囲には幅約15メートル前後、深さ約2メートルの濠がめぐっている。平坦部の古墳としては珍しい幅約5メートルの周庭（しゅうてい）が濠の外側を取り巻くように存在する。 [[古墳時代]]の中期（5世紀中頃）に周辺を勢力圏としていた豪族の丹比（たじひ）氏によって築造されたと考えられている。 [[発掘調査]]により、中世には[[砦]]として利用されていたことが判明している。 1957年（昭和32年）10月24日に国の史跡に指定された。なお1978年（昭和53年）5月6日に周庭帯部分が追加指定されている。 1989年（平成元年）度から1992年（平成4年）度にかけて'''史跡黒姫山古墳歴史の広場'''として整備され、古墳の東側には前方部の[[竪穴式石室]]と墳丘上段部の[[埴輪]]列の一部が実物大で復元展示され、前方部の一部には[[円筒埴輪]]列が復元されている。なお、出土した[[甲冑]]は、保存処理のうえ、堺市立みはら歴史博物館（M・Cみはら）に展示されている{{Cite web|和書|title=ホーム|url=http://www.mc-mihara.jp/|website=堺市立みはら歴史博物館|accessdate=2021-11-07|publisher= 堺市}}。 == 発掘調査 == [[ファイル:黒姫山古墳 甲冑群.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|甲冑（堺市指定文化財）}}{{small|堺市立みはら歴史博物館展示。}}]] 1946年（昭和21年）に前方部中央から竪穴式石室が検出されたことから、[[末永雅雄]]・[[森浩一]]らにより、戦争直後の物資の乏しい中であったが、1947年（昭和22年）12月から翌年1月にかけて第1次調査が、1948年（昭和28年）12月から翌年1月にかけて第2次調査が実施された。発掘調査成果は1958年（昭和28年）に『河内黒姫山古墳の研究』として[[大阪府教育委員会]]から出版された{{Sfn|大阪府教育委員会|1953}}。1990年（平成2年）までに第5次までの調査が行われている{{Sfn|橋本|2020|pp=7-12}}。 古墳の上段には[[円筒埴輪]]が359本並べられていた。円筒は高さ約80センチ、直径は上縁で約40センチ、下は少しすぼんでいる。また、円筒埴輪と段の縁との間に3.6メートル間隔に1つの割合で25個の以上の蓋（きぬがさ）形埴輪が置かれていた。 前方部中央の[[竪穴式石室]]から24領の甲冑が正立で2領ずつ並列した状態で出土した。この24領という甲冑の数は、1つの古墳の単一埋納施設の出土数としては、2010年（平成22年）現在で日本最多である。この石室（内法長さ4メートル、幅約0.8メートル、高さ約1メートル余り）は[[副葬品]]を納めるために、[[砂岩]]の天井石8枚で覆われ、底には川原石が敷き詰められていた。また、付属具として頸甲（あかべよろい）11、肩甲（かたよろい）12、草摺（くさずり）4、[[刀|鉄刀]]14、[[剣|鉄剣]]10、[[矛|鉄矛]]9、[[石突]]6、[[鉄鏃]]56、[[刀子]]5が出土した。 なお、古墳の被葬者が埋葬されたとみられる後円部墳頂は既に盗掘されており、埋葬施設は確認されなかった。 造り出しや前方部の斜面などの発掘で、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には[[城砦]]に利用され、防御力を大きくするためにさらに盛り土して斜面を急にし段を高くしていることなどが分かった。 黒姫山古墳 小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (M8号).JPG|小札鋲留眉庇付冑（M8号） 黒姫山古墳 小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (M9号).JPG|小札鋲留眉庇付冑（M9号） 黒姫山古墳 小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (M13号).JPG|小札鋲留眉庇付冑（M13号）
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 (S1号).JPG|横矧板鋲留衝角付冑（S1号） 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 (S3号).JPG|横矧板鋲留衝角付冑（S3号） 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 (S8号).JPG|横矧板鋲留衝角付冑（S8号） 黒姫山古墳 頸甲・肩甲 (A2号).JPG|頸甲・肩甲（A2号） 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留短甲 (T1号).JPG|横矧板鋲留短甲（T1号） 黒姫山古墳 三角板鋲留短甲 (T2号).JPG|三角板鋲留短甲（T2号） 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留短甲 (T3号)-1.JPG|横矧板鋲留短甲（T3号） 黒姫山古墳 三角板鋲留短甲 (T4号).JPG|三角板鋲留短甲（T4号） 黒姫山古墳 三角板鋲留襟付短甲 (T5号).JPG|三角板鋲留襟付短甲（T5号）
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} 黒姫山古墳 三角板鋲留短甲 (T24号).JPG|三角板鋲留短甲（T24号） == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 黒姫山古墳 - 1957年（昭和32年）10月24日指定、1978年（昭和53年）5月6日に史跡範囲の追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1803|黒姫山古墳}}。 === 堺市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 黒姫山古墳出土甲冑類（考古資料） - 堺市立みはら歴史博物館保管。2008年（平成20年）7月17日指定[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kouko/kurohimeshutsudo.html 黒姫山古墳出土甲冑類]（堺市ホームページ）。。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==参考文献== * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=大阪府教育委員会|title=河内黒姫山古墳の研究|year=1953|date=1953-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/65385|ncid=BN04020887|series=大阪府文化財調査報告書|volume=1|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=橋本達也|authorlink=橋本達也 (考古学者)|year=2020|date=2020-11-01|title=黒姫山古墳-巨大古墳の時代を解く鍵-|publisher=新泉社|isbn=9784787720375|ref=harv}} ==外部リンク== * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/kurohimeyama.html 黒姫山古墳] - 堺市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:くろひめやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:美原区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=黒姫山古墳 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[美原区]]黒山 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |築造年代=5世紀中頃 |規模=全長114[[メートル|m]]、高さ11m |被葬者=丹比氏 |史跡=[[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）国指定 |有形文化財=出土甲冑類（堺市指定文化財） |出土品=[[甲冑]]、[[鉄刀]]、鉄[[剣]]、鉄[[鏃]]ほか多数 |画像=[[画像:黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg|250px]]

Osaka|label=黒姫山古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=44.21|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=27.53|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} '''黒姫山古墳'''（くろひめやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[美原区]]黒山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定され{{Cite web|和書|author=文化観光局文化部文化財課|url=http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/kurohimeyama.html|title=黒姫山古墳|work=堺市|date=2020-04-03|accessdate=2020-10-16}}、出土甲冑類は堺市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 甲冑群.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|甲冑（堺市指定文化財）}}{{small|堺市立みはら歴史博物館展示。}}]] '''黒姫山古墳'''（くろひめやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[美原区]]黒山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定され{{Cite web|和書|author=文化観光局文化部文化財課|url=http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/kurohimeyama.html|title=黒姫山古墳|work=堺市|date=2020-04-03|accessdate=2020-10-16}}、出土甲冑類は堺市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|270px|right|{{center|黒姫山古墳の空中写真（1975年）}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[百舌鳥古墳群]]と[[古市古墳群]]の中間に位置する前方後円墳で、前方部は西側を向いている。大きさは、全長114[[メートル]]、後円部は直径64メートル・高さ11メートル、前方部は幅65メートル、高さ11.5メートルである。北側くびれ部に[[造り出し]]があり、墳丘の周囲には幅約15メートル前後、深さ約2メートルの濠がめぐっている。平坦部の古墳としては珍しい幅約5メートルの周庭（しゅうてい）が濠の外側を取り巻くように存在する。 [[古墳時代]]の中期（5世紀中頃）に周辺を勢力圏としていた豪族の丹比（たじひ）氏によって築造されたと考えられている。 [[発掘調査]]により、中世には[[砦]]として利用されていたことが判明している。 1957年（昭和32年）10月24日に国の史跡に指定された。なお1978年（昭和53年）5月6日に周庭帯部分が追加指定されている。 1989年（平成元年）度から1992年（平成4年）度にかけて'''史跡黒姫山古墳歴史の広場'''として整備され、古墳の東側には前方部の[[竪穴式石室]]と墳丘上段部の[[埴輪]]列の一部が実物大で復元展示され、前方部の一部には[[円筒埴輪]]列が復元されている。なお、出土した[[甲冑]]は、保存処理のうえ、堺市立みはら歴史博物館（M・Cみはら）に展示されている{{Cite web|和書|title=ホーム|url=http://www.mc-mihara.jp/|website=堺市立みはら歴史博物館|accessdate=2021-11-07|publisher= 堺市}}。 == 発掘調査 == [[ファイル:黒姫山古墳 甲冑群.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|甲冑（堺市指定文化財）}}{{small|堺市立みはら歴史博物館展示。}}]] 1946年（昭和21年）に前方部中央から竪穴式石室が検出されたことから、[[末永雅雄]]・[[森浩一]]らにより、戦争直後の物資の乏しい中であったが、1947年（昭和22年）12月から翌年1月にかけて第1次調査が、1948年（昭和28年）12月から翌年1月にかけて第2次調査が実施された。発掘調査成果は1958年（昭和28年）に『河内黒姫山古墳の研究』として[[大阪府教育委員会]]から出版された{{Sfn|大阪府教育委員会|1953}}。1990年（平成2年）までに第5次までの調査が行われている{{Sfn|橋本|2020|pp=7-12}}。 古墳の上段には[[円筒埴輪]]が359本並べられていた。円筒は高さ約80センチ、直径は上縁で約40センチ、下は少しすぼんでいる。また、円筒埴輪と段の縁との間に3.6メートル間隔に1つの割合で25個の以上の蓋（きぬがさ）形埴輪が置かれていた。 前方部中央の[[竪穴式石室]]から24領の甲冑が正立で2領ずつ並列した状態で出土した。この24領という甲冑の数は、1つの古墳の単一埋納施設の出土数としては、2010年（平成22年）現在で日本最多である。この石室（内法長さ4メートル、幅約0.8メートル、高さ約1メートル余り）は[[副葬品]]を納めるために、[[砂岩]]の天井石8枚で覆われ、底には川原石が敷き詰められていた。また、付属具として頸甲（あかべよろい）11、肩甲（かたよろい）12、草摺（くさずり）4、[[刀|鉄刀]]14、[[剣|鉄剣]]10、[[矛|鉄矛]]9、[[石突]]6、[[鉄鏃]]56、[[刀子]]5が出土した。 なお、古墳の被葬者が埋葬されたとみられる後円部墳頂は既に盗掘されており、埋葬施設は確認されなかった。 造り出しや前方部の斜面などの発掘で、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には[[城砦]]に利用され、防御力を大きくするためにさらに盛り土して斜面を急にし段を高くしていることなどが分かった。 黒姫山古墳 小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (M8号).JPG|小札鋲留眉庇付冑（M8号） 黒姫山古墳 小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (M9号).JPG|小札鋲留眉庇付冑（M9号） 黒姫山古墳 小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (M13号).JPG|小札鋲留眉庇付冑（M13号）
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 (S1号).JPG|横矧板鋲留衝角付冑（S1号） 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 (S3号).JPG|横矧板鋲留衝角付冑（S3号） 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 (S8号).JPG|横矧板鋲留衝角付冑（S8号） 黒姫山古墳 頸甲・肩甲 (A2号).JPG|頸甲・肩甲（A2号） 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留短甲 (T1号).JPG|横矧板鋲留短甲（T1号） 黒姫山古墳 三角板鋲留短甲 (T2号).JPG|三角板鋲留短甲（T2号） 黒姫山古墳 横矧板鋲留短甲 (T3号)-1.JPG|横矧板鋲留短甲（T3号） 黒姫山古墳 三角板鋲留短甲 (T4号).JPG|三角板鋲留短甲（T4号） 黒姫山古墳 三角板鋲留襟付短甲 (T5号).JPG|三角板鋲留襟付短甲（T5号）
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} 黒姫山古墳 三角板鋲留短甲 (T24号).JPG|三角板鋲留短甲（T24号） == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 黒姫山古墳 - 1957年（昭和32年）10月24日指定、1978年（昭和53年）5月6日に史跡範囲の追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1803|黒姫山古墳}}。 === 堺市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 黒姫山古墳出土甲冑類（考古資料） - 堺市立みはら歴史博物館保管。2008年（平成20年）7月17日指定[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kouko/kurohimeshutsudo.html 黒姫山古墳出土甲冑類]（堺市ホームページ）。。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==参考文献== * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=大阪府教育委員会|title=河内黒姫山古墳の研究|year=1953|date=1953-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/65385|ncid=BN04020887|series=大阪府文化財調査報告書|volume=1|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=橋本達也|authorlink=橋本達也 (考古学者)|year=2020|date=2020-11-01|title=黒姫山古墳-巨大古墳の時代を解く鍵-|publisher=新泉社|isbn=9784787720375|ref=harv}} ==外部リンク== * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/kurohimeyama.html 黒姫山古墳] - 堺市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:くろひめやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:美原区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kurohimeyama Kofun]][[en:Kurohimeyama Kofun]][[fr:Kofun de Kurohimeyama]][[ja:黒姫山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678079]] Wikidata name: Kurohimeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.545613888889|N|135.55764722222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Mihara-ku|qid=Q1042511}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[:en:Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|View from the west 750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Kurohimeyama Kofun001.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>

This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[:en:Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kurohimeyama Kofun]][[en:Kurohimeyama Kofun]][[fr:Kofun de Kurohimeyama]][[ja:黒姫山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678079]] Wikidata name: Kurohimeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kurohimeyama Kofun | native_name =黒姫山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:黒姫山古墳（後円部）.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.545613888889|N|135.55764722222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Mihara-ku|WD=Q1042511}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[:en:Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|View from the west 750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Kurohimeyama Kofun001.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[:en:Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[:en:Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[:en:Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|View from the west 750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Kurohimeyama Kofun001.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in [[:en:Mihara-ku, Sakai|Mihara ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Kawachi-Matsubara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers

image_size = | alt = | caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun, posterior portion with moat | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kurohimeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.545613888889|N|135.55764722222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Mihara-ku|WD=Q1042511}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|44.21|N|135|33|27.53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kurohimeyama Kofun'''|黒姫山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in {{ill|Mihara-ku, Sakai|en|Mihara-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042511|lt=Mihara ward}} of the city of {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205843 |title=黒姫山古墳|trans-title=Kurohimeyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kurohimeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the wide flat land of the Minamikawachi region between the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters, with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. There was a ceremonial platform extending off of the northern edge of the central constriction and the tumulus was surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 meters and depth of two meters. The tumulus is believed to have been associated with the Tajihi clan, a powerful tribe which controlled this area around the mid-fifth century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1946, a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was detected in the anterior rectangular portion, and this was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1947, with five more excavations occurring between 1948 and 2000. Finds included 359 cylindrical ''haniwa'', each measuring 80 centimeters in height by 40 centimeters in diameter, with a slightly recessed bottom. In addition, 25 or more lid-shaped ''haniwa'' at intervals between the cylindrical ''haniwa''. From within the burial chamber, 24 sets of armor were found, mounted in an upright position in two rows. This is the largest number of sets of armor found at any site in Japan. In addition, there were 24 iron swords, 9 iron spearheads, 6 iron stakes, 56 iron arrowheads, and 5 knives, along with other items. The burial chamber itself was four meters long and was covered by eight sandstone blocks forming the ceiling, and river stones to provide drainage on the floor. The burial chamber which was presumed to have existed in the posterior circular mound was apparently robbed in antiquity, and there is no trace remaining.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The shape of the tumulus was modified when it was used as a fortification in the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, by cutting away the sides to make the slope steeper.
From 1989 the tumulus and its surroundings have been maintained as the Kurohimeyama Kofun historical plaza, and the pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] on the east side of the tumulus and a part of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' row in the upper part of the Kofun have been restored. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Sakai Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Matsubara Station|en|Kawachi-Matsubara Station|WD=Q6379458|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: dd4 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 65 meters wide x dd.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 64 meter diameter x dd meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 黒姫山古墳（前方部）.jpg|View from the west 750124 Kurohimeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Kurohimeyama Kofun001.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Kurohimeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/kurohimeyama.html Sakai City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://osaka-info.jp/spot/kurohigeyama-tumulus/ Osaka Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
